# REQ-FR-260011: GraphQL-backed authentication with Google Sign-In and demo access

- ID: FR-260011
- Class: FR (Functional Requirement)
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-06-10
- Owner: Repository owner

## Statement

The public site at `crystord.com` shall authenticate users — sign-in, sign-up, and Google
Sign-In — by calling the backend GraphQL API directly, and shall provide one-click demo access
using the shared demo account (`demo` / `demo`).

## Rationale

The backend GraphQL schema exposes `signin`, `signup`, `signinGoogle`, and `getGoogleClientID`
operations. The public site currently uses a legacy REST endpoint for sign-in only. Connecting
the full auth surface closes the gap between what the backend offers and what the site delivers,
and removes the detour to `app.crystord.com` for new-user signup and Google SSO.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Sign-in (email / password)

- The sign-in form on the landing page and `/sign-in` calls
  `query signin(email, password)` on `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL`.
- On success: the browser navigates to `https://app.crystord.com/?token=<jwt>`.
- On failure: an inline error message is shown; no full page reload occurs.
- The GraphQL endpoint URL is read from `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL` at build time; it is not
  hardcoded in any page or component.

### Sign-up (email / password)

- A `/sign-up` page exists on the public site with a form for email, password, and optional
  username.
- The form calls `mutation signup(email, password, username)` on `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL`.
- On success: the browser navigates to `https://app.crystord.com/?token=<jwt>`.
- On failure: an inline error message is shown; no full page reload occurs.

### Google Sign-In

- A Google Sign-In button appears on both the sign-in and sign-up pages.
- The button is rendered using the Google Identity Services (GSI) library loaded from
  `accounts.google.com/gsi/client`.
- The OAuth client ID is fetched at page load via `query getGoogleClientID` on
  `PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL`.
- On successful Google authentication: `query signinGoogle(idToken)` is called; on success
  the browser navigates to `app.crystord.com/#token=<jwt>`.
- If `getGoogleClientID` fails the button is not rendered; the email/password path remains
  fully functional.

### Demo access

- A "Try Demo" button on the sign-in form submits with the demo credentials (`demo` / `demo`)
  through the standard sign-in flow.
- The demo credentials are not exposed in HTML markup.

### Test coverage

- Unit tests cover 100% of `auth-graphql.ts` and `google-auth.ts` utility modules.
- Existing Playwright e2e suite passes with no regressions.
- Boundary tests pass; the GSI script origin is listed as a permitted exception.
